detaileddescription.para: An unspecified alignment value.
Normal alignment relative to text direction.
Centered alignment.
Opposite alignment relative to text direction.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Cue text alignment within the cue box.

detaileddescription.para: An unset line type value.
Line position is fractional position within the viewport.
Line position is number of lines, interpretation also depends on the line anchor type.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
An unset anchor type value.
Anchors the left (for horizontal positions) or top (for vertical positions) edge of the cue box.
Anchors the middle of the cue box.
Anchors the right (for horizontal positions) or bottom (for vertical positions) edge of the cue box.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
An unset text size type value.
Text size is measured as a fraction of the viewport size minus the view padding.
Text size is measured as a fraction of the viewport size, ignoring the view padding.
Text size is measured in number of pixels.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Cue text alignment within the cue box.
An unset position, width or size which is negative of maximum finite value of float (-FLT_MAX).

memberdef.argsstring: ((deprecated("Use standard Android SDK APIs."))) struct MLCea608CaptionStyleColor
memberdef.name: __attribute__

detaileddescription.para: Restarts the MediaRecorder to its idle state.
Release the surface that was returned by MLMediaRecorderGetInputSurface.
Stops recording.
Returns the maximum absolute amplitude that was sampled since the last call to this method.
Gets the input surface to record from when using SURFACE video source.
Begins capturing and encoding data to the specified file.
Prepares the recorder to begin capturing and encoding data for input mediaformat.
Sets the GEO location for recording.
Sets the maximum filesize (in bytes) of the recording session.
Sets the maximum duration (in ms) of the recording session.
Sets the audio encoder to be used for recording.
Sets the video encoder to be used for recording.
Sets the format of the output file produced during recording.
Set MediaRecorder audio source.
Sets MediaRecorder default video source.
Sets the MediaRecorderListener object that will receive MediaRecorder notifications.
Sets the path of the output file to be produced.
Pass in the file descriptor of the file to be written.
Destroy a MediaRecorder object.
Create a new MediaRecorder object.
WebVTT data structure.
TTML data structure.
Byte data for TTML image cues.
Sets the Native surface to be used as the sink for displaying the video portion of the media.
Retrieves metrics recorded on the MediaPlayer.
MediaPlayer metrics data.
Resets the Media Player to its uninitialized state asynchronously.
Gets TTML data represented by a timed text handle.
Gets WebVTT data represented by a timed text handle.
Set callback to get invoked when a Timed Text update is available along with its data.
Sets buffering settings.
Gets current Buffering settings.
Gets default Buffering settings.
Releases DRM.
Prepares DRM for the selected media (either audio or video) track.
Gets the DRM info of the selected media (either audio or video) track.
Polls the desired states from internal Media Player.
Sets the volume on this player.
Sets the looping mode of the player.
Sets video scaling mode.
Returns last metadata event information.
Sets callback to get notified when a subtitle update is available along with its data.
Releases last subtitle event information.
Returns last subtitle event information.
Returns last timed text event information.
Returns last error received by internal Media Player.
Returns last info received by internal Media Player.
Returns current buffering percentage.
Returns media duration.
Returns current position of playback.
Returns the size of the video frame.
Sets callbacks to notify clients about player events.
Seeks to specified time position.
Resets the Media Player to its uninitialized state.
Pauses playback.
Stops playback after playback has been started or paused.
Starts or resumes playback.
Prepares the player for playback, asynchronously.
Prepares the player for playback, synchronously.
Unselects a track.
Selects a track.
Gets the Media Format of a track.
Gets the language of a track.
Gets the type of a track.
Counts the number of tracks found in the data source.
Sets the 'next' Media Player to automatically 'start' when current Media Player playback completes.
Adds a TimedText source from a file descriptor.
Adds a TimedText source from a URI.
Sets the data source to use.
Sets a URI string as the remote data source.
Sets a URI string as the data source.
Sets a local file path as the data source.
Sets a file descriptor as the data source with offset.
Sets a file descriptor as the data source.
Destroys a MediaPlayer object.
Create a new Media Player object.
DRM Session information for a MediaPlayer Track.
MediaPlayer Track Information.
Callbacks for notifying client about Media Player error events.
MediaPlayer frame packing callback Info.
MediaPlayer reset completion callback Info.
MediaPlayer Track DRM Info callback Info.
MediaPlayer Video Size Changed callback Info.
MediaPlayer Playback seek completion callback Info.
MediaPlayer Playback prepared callback Info.
MediaPlayer Information callback Info.
MediaPlayer Error callback Info.
MediaPlayer Playback completion callback Info.
MediaPlayer Buffering update callback Info.
MediaPlayer DRM Info for a Media Track .
Destroy a #MediaDataSource object.
Create a new MediaDataSource object.
Set callback to get notified when a caption event has generated.
Release Cea708 parser object.
Provide Cea708 parser with subtitle raw data for parsing.
Create Cea708 parser object.
Cea708 Caption Event.
Cea708 Caption Window.
Cea708 Caption Window Attributes.
Cea708 Caption Pen Location.
Cea708 Caption Pen Color.
Cea708 Caption Pen Attributes.
Cea708 Caption Color.
Provide Cea608 parser with subtitle raw data for parsing. If the callback has been set by MLMediaCCParserSetDisplayChangedCallback, then its issued to notify the CC Data.
Set callback to get notified when a caption displayable has changed.
Release internal CC parser object.
Return closed caption for display.
Create internal closed caption parser object.
Cea608 caption segment structure.
Cea608 caption line structure.
Cea608 caption preamble address code.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to an MLHandle which will contain the handle to the CC parser. If this operation fails, out_handle will be ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
Internal closed caption parser object was created successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
A handle to a CC parser.
Subtitle data of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData structure returned from MLMediaPlayerGetSubtitleEx function.
Subtitle data size of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData structure returned from MLMediaPlayerGetSubtitleEx function.
The returned MLCea608CaptionSegmentEx pointer for CC display. NOTE that user doesn't own this memory.
one of the arguements is incorrect.
CC is ready to display and content is stored in out_ccSeg.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
CC parser was not properly built or initialized.
more data is required and no displayable CC for now.
A handle to a CC parser.
internal CC parser object was released successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: Setting this callback saves application from handling raw subtitle data. Caption will be delivered in this callback at displayable time.

parameterdescription.para: A handle to a CC parser.

Function pointer for display changed callback. See MLMediaCCParserDisplayChangedCallback() callback typedef declaration and comments for explanation on expected callback parameters. This can be 'NULL' to unset the callback.
Custom data to be returned when callback is fired.
Passed cc_handle handle was not found.
Callback was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
A handle to a Cea608 parser.
Subtitle data of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData.
Subtitle data size of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData.
the input parameter(s) is(are) wrong.
the operation was successful.
Cea708 parser was not properly built or initialized.
A pointer to an MLHandle which will contain the handle to the Cea708 parser. If this operation fails, out_handle will be ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
The operation failed with memory allocation error.
out_handle is null.
Internal Cea708 parser object was created successfully.
A handle to a Cea708 parser.
Subtitle data of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData.
Subtitle data size of MLMediaPlayerSubtitleData.
the input parameter(s) is(are) wrong.
the operation was successful.
Cea708 parser was not properly built or initialized.
A handle to a Cea708 parser.
cc_handle is wrong or not found.
internal Cea708 parser object was released successfully.
A handle to a Cea708 parser.

Function pointer for event callback. See MLMediaCea708ParserEmitEventCallback() callback declaration and comments for explanation on expected callback parameters. This can be 'NULL' to unset the callback.
Custom data to be returned when callback is fired.
Passed cc_handle handle was not found.
Callback was successfully set.
MLMediaDataSourceReadAt callback.
MLMediaDataSourceGetSize callback.
MLMediaDataSourceClose callback.
User data to be passed to callbacks.
The MLHandle to the new source object created. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
One of the parameters is invalid.
if operation was successful.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
MLHandle to the #MediaDataSource object to destroy.
One of the parameters is invalid.
if operation was successful.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para:
API Level:\n 2
MediaPlayer subtitle data.
Indicate the buffering settings.
Those are async mechanisms that should not be used in parallel with their sync counterpart, i.e. poll-based queries for the same events using MLMediaPlayerPollStatespoll().

simplesect.para: This is a blocking sync method. As creating a MediaPlayer object might take some time, make sure to not call this API from your main UI thread.

parameterdescription.para: The MLHandle to the new Media Player object created. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully created MediaPlayer.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

simplesect.para: This is a blocking sync method. As destroying a MediaPlayer object might take some time, make sure to not call this API from your main UI thread.

parameterdescription.para: MLHandle to the Media Player object to destroy.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully destroyed MediaPlayer.
Failed due to an unknown error.

detaileddescription.para: The file descriptor must be seekable. It is the caller's responsibility to close the file descriptor. It is safe to do so as soon as this call returns.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The file descriptor for the file you want to play.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set file descriptor.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: The file descriptor must be seekable. It is the caller's responsibility to close the file descriptor. It is safe to do so as soon as this call returns. This API is useful for specifying playable media located in resource files.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The file descriptor for the file you want to play.
The offset (in bytes) of the source's start.
The number of bytes of media to load after offset.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set file descriptor.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: The path should be an absolute path and should reference a world-readable file.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The linux-style path of the file.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set local file path.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Supported URI schemes are file, http, https, and rtsp. If looking to provide headers, use MLMediaPlayerSetRemoteDataSourceForURI().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The C-style string representing the URI complete string.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set URI string.
Failed due to lack of permission.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Supported URI schemes are file, http, https, and rtsp.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The C-style string representing the complete URL.
Array of char * headers to be sent.
Number of headers passed.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set URI string.
Failed due to lack of permission.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
MLHandle returned by MLMediaDataSourceCreate().
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set the data source to use.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because no valid source could be found.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
MLHandle returned by MLMediaStreamSourceCreate().
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set the data source to use.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because no valid source could be found.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

The C-style string representing the complete URI.
The MIME type of the file.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully added TimedText source from URI.
Failed due to an unknown failure.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: The file descriptor must be seekable. It is the caller's responsibility to close the file descriptor. It is safe to do so as soon as this call returns.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The file descriptor for the timed text source.
The offset (in bytes) of the source's start. Must be >= 0.
The number of bytes of media to load after offset. Must be >= 0.
The MIME type of the file.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully added TimedText source from file descriptor.
Failed due to an unknown failure.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
MLHandle of an already created Media Player instance. To 'remove' the next player, set next_media_player = 0.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully registered next Media Player.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed source because no valid source could be found.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The number of tracks found in the player source.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the number of tracks.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The track number for which type is to be returned. Has to be < TrackCount.
The returned type for specified track number.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned type for passed track.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Found track's type was unknown.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The track number for which language is to be returned. Has to be < TrackCount.
The returned language of the track. Caller has ownership of that param and has to free it when no longer needed.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned language for passed track.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The track number for which Media Format is to be returned. Has to be < TrackCount.
MLHandle to MediaFormat of the track. Caller has ownership of this handle and should call #MLMediaFormatDestroy to free it when no longer needed.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully retreived the media format of passed track.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Number of the track to select. Has to be < TrackCount.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully selected track.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Number of the track to unselect. Has to be < TrackCount.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully unselected track.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: After setting the data source and the #GraphicBufferProducer, you need to either call prepare() or prepareAsync(). For files, it is OK to call prepare(), which blocks until Media Player is ready for playback.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully prepared the player.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. Can only be called after one of the setDataSource methods or in the 'stopped' state.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Failed becaue MediaPlayer could not connect with the given URL.

detaileddescription.para: After setting the data's source and the #GraphicBufferProducer, you need to either call prepare() or prepareAsync(). For streams, you should call prepareAsync(), which returns immediately, rather than blocking until enough data has been buffered. Prepared state will then be obtained via the 'on_prepared' callback if already registered (see MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx), or polled for via the MLMediaPlayerPollStates() call with the MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasBeenPrepared flag set.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully prepared the player asynchronously. For result, poll for MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasBeenPrepared state or wait for 'on_prepared' callback if already set.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. Can only be called after one of the setDataSource methods or in the 'stopped' state.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: If playback had previously been paused, playback will continue from where it was paused. If playback had been stopped, or never started before, playback will start at the beginning.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully started/resumed playback.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. Can only be called in one of the following states: 'prepared', 'playback complete', 'paused', 'stopped'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully stopped playback.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. Can only be called in one of the following states: 'playing', 'paused', 'stopped', 'prepared', 'playback complete'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Calling pause() is a NOOP if Media Player is in state #MEDIA_PLAYER_PAUSED or #MEDIA_PLAYER_PLAYBACK_COMPLETE.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully paused playback.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. Can only be called in one of the following states: 'playing', 'paused', 'playback complete'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: After calling this method, you will have to initialize it again by setting the data source and calling prepare().

simplesect.para: This is a blocking sync method, make sure to not call this API from your main UI thread.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully reset MediaPlayer.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. This method can be called anytime except when in 'preparing async'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Note that SeekTo is an async. function and returns immediately. Successful seek result has to be obtained either via the 'on_seek_complete' if already registered (see MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx) or by polling for the flag 'MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasSeekCompleted' when calling MLMediaPlayerPollStates().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The offset in milliseconds from the start, to seek to.
Seek mode defined by MLMediaSeekMode.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully launched seek operation asynchronously. For result, poll for MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasSeekCompleted state or wait for the 'on_seek_complete' callback if already set.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. This method can only be called from the following states: 'started', 'prepared', 'paused' and 'playback complete'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: This structure must be initialized by calling MLMediaPlayerEventCallbacksExInit() before use.
Client needs to implement the callback functions declared in MLMediaPlayerEventCallbacksEx, but can set to NULL the ones he does not care about.

parameterdescription.para: MLHandle to the MediaCodec.
Set of event callbacks. Can be set to NULL to unset the callbacks altogether.
Custom data to be returned when any callback is fired.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set/unset the specified callbacks.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned video width.
The returned video height.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned video size.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned position in milliseconds.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned current position.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned duration in milliseconds.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the media duration.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. This method can only be called from the following states: 'prepared', 'started', 'paused', 'stopped' and 'playback complete'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned buffering percentage in the range [0, 100].
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned current buffering percentage.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The main info code.
The secondary code/data.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the last MediaPlayer.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The result code.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the last MediaPlayer error.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The text of the timed text event.
The start time.
The end time.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the last timedtext entry.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned MLMediaPlayerGetSubtitleExArgs pointer.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the last subtitle entry.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully released the last subtitle entry.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para:
API Level:\n 4
Setting this callback disables use of the poll-based API to query for the MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasSubtitleUpdated flag. MLMediaPlayerPollStates() will ignore checks for this bit.
This also makes MLMediaPlayerGetSubtitleEx() redundant as the subtitle data is provided with the callback.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.

Function pointer to be called. This can be 'NULL' to unset the callback. See MLMediaPlayerOnMediaSubtitleUpdateCallback() callback typedef declaration and comments for explanation on expected callback parameters.
Custom data to be returned when callback is fired.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set callback.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The returned event time.
The returned event buffer size.
The returned event buffer. This buffer is only valid until the next metadata event arrives, so make a copy if you need to use the buffer later.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned last metadata entry.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
MLHandle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The video scaling mode to set. For a list of valid values, see #media_video_scaling_mode_type declaration.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned current video scaling mode.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. SetDataSource must be called immediately after creation, or after a media player reset.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The looping mode. Set to true if player should loop to beginning of MediaPlayer when EOS reached. Set to false if MediaPlayer should simply stop playback at end of media.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned current looping mode.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The new volume in the [0.0, 1.0] range.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set the volume.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: Desired states have to be selected by passing a bitwise OR'ed mask of MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlags flags.
The "is_XXX" states can be polled multiples times and the return value will be the same if internal state hasn't changed since last call.
When polling the "has_XXX" states however, internal state is set to false, since they mean : has <state> happened/changed since I last polled <state>.
Two-step state polling : The following states, when returning '1', indicate there is some data ready to be retrieved, by calling a getter function, as explained below.
For '#MLMediaPlayerPollingState_HasSizeChanged', call MLMediaPlayerGetVideoSize() to get the new size.
For '#MLMediaPlayerPollingState_HasBufferingUpdated', call MLMediaPlayerGetBufferingUpdate() to get buffering progress value.
For '#MLMediaPlayerPollingState_HasReceivedInfo, call MLMediaPlayerGetError() to get last info code and data.
For '#MLMediaPlayerPollingState_HasReceivedError, call MLMediaPlayerGetError() to get last error code and data.
Note: This API can still be used even if an #OnBufferAvailable callback has been set using MLMediaPlayerSetOnBufferAvailableCallback() method.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Selection mask for picking which states to retrieve.
Bitwise OR'ed integer of all the chosen states from bitwise 'mask' and in the range [0 = false : 1 = true].
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully polled and returned the selected states.
Failed due to an unknown error.

detaileddescription.para: This function has to be called only after DataSource has been set and the MediaPlayer is completely prepared.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Media track information defined by MLMediaPlayerTrackInfo.
Pointer to the pointer to MLMediaPlayerTrackDRMInfo struct. The caller should not free the pointer returned. The memory will be released in the call to MLMediaPlayerReleaseDRM().
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned the current DRM Info.
Media Source is not prepared yet. Wait for on_prepared callback and call again.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
DRM Information not available for the given track.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
DRM Session info for the Media track, as defined by MLMediaPlayerTrackDRMSessionInfo.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully prepared DRM.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully released DRM.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: \Attention The API is only implemented in ML1.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
A pointer to MLMediaPlayerBufferingSettings.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned default Buffering settings.
Feature not implemented in the given model.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: \Attention The API is only implemented in ML1.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
A pointer to MLMediaPlayerBufferingSettings.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned current Buffering settings.
Feature not implemented in the given model.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: \Attention The API is only implemented in ML1.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
An MLMediaPlayerBufferingSettings struct.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully set buffering settings.
Feature not implemented in the given model.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para:
API Level:\n 4
Setting this callback disables use of the poll-based API to query for the MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasTimedTextUpdated flag. MLMediaPlayerPollStates() will ignore checks for this bit.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Function pointer to be called. This can be 'NULL' to unset the callback.
Custom data to be returned when callback is fired.
Successfully set callback.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Failed due to an unknown error.

detaileddescription.para: The returned MLWebVTTData structure is valid only in the callback's context.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The Timed Text handle.
The WebVTT data structure to be returned.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned WebVTT data.
Failed because the timed_text handle was not in the WebVTT format. The timed text track's mime type must be "text/vtt".
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: The returned MLTTMLData structure is valid only in the callback's context.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
The Timed Text handle.
The TTML data structure to be returned.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully returned TTML data.
Failed because the timed_text handle was not in the TTML format. The timed text track's mime type must be "application/ttml+xml".
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: This method is asynchronous and will return immediately. After invocation, you will have to initialize it again by setting the data source and calling prepare() or prepareAsync().

A successful reset result can be obtained either by registering the 'on_reset_complete' callback (see MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx) or by polling for the 'MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasResetCompleted' flag when calling MLMediaPlayerPollStates().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully launched MediaPlayer reset operation asynchronously. For result, poll for MLMediaPlayerPollingStateFlag_HasResetCompleted or use the 'on_reset_complete' callback if already set.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state. This method can be called anytime except when in 'preparing async'.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.
Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
See MLMediaPlayerMetrics.
Failed due to an invalid parameter.
Successfully received metrics.
Failed due to an unknown error.
Failed because method was called in the wrong state.
Failed because MediaPlayer was not properly initialized.

detaileddescription.para: The surface should be created by MLNativeSurfaceCreate(). When called, this method should be called after one of the MLMediaPlayerSetDataSource() API and before MLMediaPlayerPrepare() or MLMediaPlayerPrepareAsync() Not calling this method or calling this with surface as ML_INVALID_HANDLE will result in only the audio track being played.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaPlayer instance.
Handle to the MLNativeSurface object.
Passed media_player or the surface handle was not found.
Successfully set output surface.
Internal error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
MediaPlayer was not properly built or initialized.
Handle to the new MediaRecorder object created. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
Could not allocate internal MediaRecorder.
Passed out_handle was NULL.
MediaRecorder was created successfully.
Handle to the media recorder object to destroy.
Media recorder handle was not found.
MediaRecorder was destroyed successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() but before MLMediaRecorderprepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder instance.
The file descriptor for the file you want to write into.
Passed fd does no refer to a valid,open file descriptor or 'media_recorder' was NULL.
Data source was correctly set to passed file descriptor.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() but before MLMediaRecorderprepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder instance.
The linux-style path of the file. Must be a valid and existing path.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Data source was correctly set to passed file path.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
Set of event callbacks. Can be set to NULL to unset the callbacks altogether.
Custom data to be returned when any callback is fired.
Media recorder handle was not found.
The specified callbacks were successfully set/unset.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Cannot be called twice (without calling MLMediaRecorderReset() in between) because it triggers internal initialization.
Current implementation supports only camera as video source from MediaRecorderVideoSource.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MediaRecorderVideoSource.
Media recorder handle was not found or in_video_source is not MLMediaRecorderVideoSource_Camera.
Set default video source successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Cannot be called twice (without calling MLMediaRecorderReset() in between) because it triggers internal initialization.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MediaRecorderAudioSource.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set audio source successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MLMediaRecorderOutputFormat.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set output format successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: If this method is not called, the output file will not contain an video track. Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare().
The video source is always set to camera by default.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the VideoEncoder to use.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set video encoder successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
requested width height is not supported.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: If this method is not called, the output file will not contain an audio track. Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MLMediaRecorderAudioEncoder to use.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set audio encoder successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). After recording reaches the specified duration, a notification will be sent via the callback with a MLMediaRecorderInfo code of MLMediaRecorderInfo_MaxDurationReached and recording will be stopped. Stopping happens asynchronously, there is no guarantee that the recorder will have stopped by the time the listener is notified.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
The maximum duration in ms (if zero or negative, disables the duration limit).
Media recorder handle was not found.
Max duration was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para:
API Level:\n 20
Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). After recording reaches the specified filesize, a notification will be sent via the callback with a MLMediaRecorderInfo code of MLMediaRecorderInfo_MaxFileSizeReached and recording will be stopped. Stopping happens asynchronously, there is no guarantee that the recorder will have stopped by the time the listener is notified.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
MaxFilesizeBytes The maximum filesize in bytes (if zero or negative, disables the limit).
Media recorder handle was not found.
Max file size was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
The 10000 multiplies latitude of location.
The 10000 multiplies longitude of location.
Media recorder handle was not found.
GEO location was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: This method must be called after setting up the desired audio and video sources, encoders, but before start.

API Level:\n 20
media_recorder
Handle to the media recorder.
format
MLHandle to the MediaFormat. MediaFormat object can be created with:
#MLMediaFormatCreateVideo if we want to record video only.
#MLMediaFormatCreateAudio if we want to record audio only.
#MLMediaFormatCreate if we want to record both video and audio.
Following parameters can be added to MediaFormat handle. Audio Specific parametes:
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Bit_Rate for audio "bitrate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Channel_Count for audio "channel-count".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Sample_Rate for audio "sample-rate".

Video Specific parametes:
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Width for video frame "width".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Height for video frame "height".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Frame_Rate for video "frame-rate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Parameter_Video_Bitrate for "video-bitrate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_I_Frame_Interval for "i-frame-interval".

parameterdescription.para: Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was prepared successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). The apps should not start another recording session during recording.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was started successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: May only be called after MLMediaRecorderPrepare. Frames rendered to the producer before MLMediaRecorderStart() is called will be discarded.
When using an input surface, there are no accessible input buffers, as buffers are automatically passed from the other modules to this surface.

The returned input surface can also be passed as a destination surface to
a video/mixed reality video capture session when calling MLCameraPrepareCapture().

Captured raw video frames will be consumed directly as input to an encoder without copying. Caller of this API should release the surface using MLMediaRecorderReleaseInputSurface() on the Surface handle after usage.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the Media Recorder instance.
Handle to the created input native surface.
Media recorder handle was not found.
input surface was correctly returned.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this only after the MLMediaRecorderSetAudioSource().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaRecorder.
The maximum absolute amplitude measured since the last call, or 0 when called for the first time.
Mmedia recorder handle was not found.
Maximum amplitude was returned successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderStart(). Once recording is stopped, you will have to configure it again as if it has just been constructed.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaRecorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was stopped successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the MediaRecorder.
Handle to the internal surface object.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Surface object was successfully released.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
MLMediaCodec was not initialized.

detaileddescription.para: After calling this method, you will have to configure it again as if it had just been constructed.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was resetted successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

inbodydescription.para: Emitted Cea708 caption event type.
Emitted Cea708 caption event specific data, based on the event type.

If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_Buffer, obj will point to a null terminated string of maximum size MLCea708CaptionEmitCommandBuffer_MaxSize bytes. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_Control, obj will point to one byte character. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_CWX, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_CLW, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_DSW, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_HDW, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_TGW, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_DLW, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_DLY, obj will point to an integer. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_DLC, obj will be NULL. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_RST, obj will be NULL. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_SPA, obj will point to MLCea708CaptionPenAttr. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_SPC, obj will point to MLCea708CaptionPenColor. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_SPL, obj will point to MLCea708CaptionPenLocation. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_SWA, obj will point to MLCea708CaptionWindowAttr. If the type is MLCea708CaptionEmitCommand_DFX, obj will point to MLCea708CaptionWindow.
Number of PSSH entries.
Pointer to array of MLMediaPlayerPSSHEntry of size pssh_info_count.
Number of supported DRM UUID entries.
Pointer to array of MLUUID of size uuid_count.
Media track type, can be either audio or video.
For prepare.
For playback.
Time based.
Size based.
When cached data is below this mark, playback will be paused for buffering until data reaches |mRebufferingWatermarkHighMs| or end of stream.
When cached data is above this mark, buffering will be paused.
When cached data is below this mark, playback will be paused for buffering until data reaches |mRebufferingWatermarkHighKB| or end of stream.
When cached data is above this mark, buffering will be paused.
The new percentage of buffered content.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
Error/result code indicating failure reason.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
Type of informational event.
MLMediaPlayerInfo type specific extra information.
When info is MLMediaPlayerInfo_NetworkBandwidth, this holds bandwidth in kbps. It is 0 for others.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
The new surface width.
The new surface height.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerTrackDRMInfo.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().

API Level:\n 8
Frame packing more.
Frame packing flag.
User data as passed to MLMediaPlayerSetEventCallbacksEx().
This callback function is invoked when buffered contents percentage changed.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
update_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnBufferingUpdateInfo.
This callback is invoked when media player played back until end of media and has now come to a stop.
Note that this callback does not fire when 'looping = true', because Media Player does not "stop" in that case, but rather loops to beginning of media.
To get notified when EOS is reached (when looping = true), set a proper callback function for the below on_info and look for the #MEDIA_PLAYER_INFO_LOOPING info code.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
completion_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnCompletionInfo.
This callback function is invoked when media player encounters an error.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
erro_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnErrorInfo.
This callback function is invoked when Media Player generates informational events.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnInfoInfo.
This callback is invoked when the player has finished preparing media and is ready to playback.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
prepared_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnPreparedInfo.
This callback function is invoked when a seek operation has completed.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
seek_complete_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnSeekCompleteInfo.
This callback function is invoked when the internal surface has changed size.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
video_size_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnVideoSizeChangedInfo.
This callback function is invoked when source has DRM protected media track(s).

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
track_drm_info
Pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnTrackDRMInfo.
This callback function is invoked when an async reset operation has completed.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
reset_complete_info
pointer to MLMediaPlayerOnResetCompleteInfo.
This callback function is invoked when a stereoscopic video frame packing change.

media_player
MLHandle is a Media Player instance on which callback was set.
mode
A MLMediaFramePackingMode.
flags
A MLMediaFramePackingFlags.
Media track type, can be either audio or video.
Media track type, can be either audio or video.
UUID of the DRM Scheme of type MLUUID.
DRM Session ID of type MLMediaDRMByteArray.
The average framerate (fps) of video playback.
The average bitrate (bps) of video playback.
The average bitrate (bps) of audio playback.
Image size in bytes.
Byte data of the image.
Track cue start time in milliseconds.
Track cue end time in milliseconds.
The cue text encoded as UTF-8. Or null if this is an image cue.
The alignment of the cue text within the cue box.
The cue image if this is an image cue, an empty image otherwise.
The position of the #line_anchor of the cue box within the viewport in the direction orthogonal to the writing direction, or MLTTMLDimen_Unset.

When set, the interpretation of the value depends on the value of #line_type. For horizontal text and #line_type equal to MLTTMLLineType_Fraction, this is the fractional vertical position relative to the top of the viewport.
The cue line type.
MLTTMLLineType_Fraction indicates that #line is a fractional position within the viewport.

MLTTMLLineType_Number indicates that #line is a line number, where the size of each line is taken to be the size of the first line of the cue. When #line is greater than or equal to 0 lines count from the start of the viewport, with 0 indicating zero offset from the start edge. When #line is negative lines count from the end of the viewport, with -1 indicating zero offset from the end edge. For horizontal text the line spacing is the height of the first line of the cue, and the start and end of the viewport are the top and bottom respectively.
Note that it's particularly important to consider the effect of #line_anchor when using MLTTMLLineType_Number.
(line==0&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_Start) Positions a (potentially multi-line) cue at the very top of the viewport.
(line==-1&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_EndD) Positions a (potentially multi-line) cue at the very bottom of the viewport.
(line==0&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_End) and (line==-1&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_Start) Position cues entirely outside of the viewport.
(line==1&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_End) Positions a cue so that only the last line is visible at the top of the viewport.
(line==-2&&line_anchor==MLTTMLAnchorType_Start) Position a cue so that only its first line is visible at the bottom of the viewport.
The cue box anchor in the direction of line.
The fractional position of the #position_anchor of the cue box within the viewport in the direction orthogonal to #line, or MLTTMLDimen_Unset.
For horizontal text, this is the horizontal position relative to the left of the viewport. Note that positioning is relative to the left of the viewport even in the case of right-to-left text.
The cue box anchor in the direction of position.
The cue box size in the writing direction, as a fraction of the viewport size or MLTTMLDimen_Unset.
The cue bitmap height as a fraction of the viewport size or MLTTMLDimen_Unset if the bitmap should be displayed at its natural height given the bitmap dimensions and the specified size.
Specifies whether or not the #window_color property is set.
The cue window fill color in ARGB format.
The cue default text size type, or MLTTMLTextSizeType_Unset if this cue has no default text size.
The cue default text size, or MLTTMLDimen_Unset if this cue has no default.
Track cue start time in milliseconds.
Track cue end time in milliseconds.
WebVTT file body encoded as UTF-8.
A sequence of characters unique amongst all the WebVTT cue identifiers.
A boolean indicating whether the line is an integer number of lines.
Orientation of the cue.
The writing direction.
Relative cue line position.
WebVTT cue line number.
The indent of the cue box in the direction defined by the writing direction.
WebVTT cue size.
WebVTT cue text alignment.
